title: Dog Eyelid Rolling Inward (Entropion): Symptoms and Triage Guide
meta_desc: Is your dog's eyelid rolling inward? Learn about Entropion, its urgency level, and why a photo is vital for veterinary triage.
content_html:
TL;DR: Entropion is a painful condition where a dog's eyelid rolls inward, requiring veterinary care within 24 to 48 hours to prevent corneal ulcers and potential vision loss.

What is entropion in dogs and how does it affect their eyes?

Entropion is a medical condition where a dog's eyelid rolls inward, causing the fur and eyelashes to rub directly against the surface of the eye (the cornea). This friction is extremely uncomfortable and leads to irritation, redness, and excessive tearing. Over time, the constant rubbing can cause corneal ulcers, which are painful sores that may lead to permanent scarring or loss of vision if not addressed.

Is dog entropion considered a veterinary emergency?

  • The urgency level for this condition is Medium; while rarely life-threatening, it requires veterinary care within 24 to 48 hours.
  • Seek a veterinary appointment as soon as possible if the eye appears cloudy or if there is thick yellow or green discharge.
  • Prompt care is necessary if your dog is pawing aggressively at the eye to prevent secondary infections or deep ulcerations.

How can providing a photo of my dog's eye help the triage process?

  • A clear, well-lit photo helps veterinary professionals assess the severity of the eyelid fold.
  • Visuals allow for the identification of corneal cloudiness (edema) and physical damage to the eye surface.
  • This information helps determine if your pet needs an immediate urgent visit or a standard scheduled appointment.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

Entropion, or inward rolling of the eyelid, is a common inherited eyelid defect in many canine breeds, but can also result from cicatrix formation and blepharospasm due to ocular or periocular pain. Inversion of cilia or facial hairs can cause conjunctival and corneal irritation, potentially leading to corneal scarring, pigmentation, and ulceration if left unaddressed. Early spastic entropion may be reversed by removing the inciting cause or alleviating pain through everting the lid hairs with mattress sutures, subcutaneous injections (e.g., procaine penicillin), or palpebral nerve blocks. Temporary stay sutures or surgical staples for 2-3 weeks can be used in very young puppies, while established entropion typically requires surgical correction.

Chapter: Ophthalmology, Surgery

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 489)

faqs.question: Can I use human eye drops to help my dog?
faqs.answer: No, never use human eye medications without consulting a vet. Some ingredients can worsen eye issues or cause severe reactions in dogs.

faqs.question: Will my dog need surgery for a rolling eyelid?
faqs.answer: In many cases, yes. Corrective surgery is the most common long-term solution to fix the eyelid position and prevent chronic pain.

faqs.question: What can I do to help my dog right now?
faqs.answer: The most important step is to place an Elizabethan collar (cone) on your dog to prevent them from rubbing or scratching the eye, which could cause a rupture.
